facade_colour: black-painted commercial block beside a faded white-painted brick heritage row with mixed illuminated storefront signs

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: black-painted commercial block beside a faded white-painted brick heritage row with mixed illuminated storefront signs
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: The block is occupied but visually uneven. The black-painted portion looks renovated while the adjoining white heritage section shows peeling paint, patched masonry, and varied storefront conditions.
photo_observations.window_details: Large arched upper openings appear on the black section, while the white heritage row has arched and rectangular upper windows with mixed infill and replacement units.
photo_observations.door_details: Multiple storefront entries line the sidewalk; some are recessed, some shuttered, and some brightly lit.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Parapet roofline survives in simplified form; original trim is largely obscured by paint and alteration.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible at night.
photo_observations.decorative_details: The heritage section retains arched openings, vestigial decorative brickwork, and shaped parapet elements despite heavy wear.
photo_observations.storefront_description: A continuous strip of small retail units runs behind the sidewalk and TTC shelter, with layered illuminated signs, mixed recessed entries, and uneven storefront treatments that read as a heavily altered commercial row.
photo_observations.signage_observed: Jump+, Awesome Tteokbokki, Medicine Wheel, and other storefront signs are visible across the block.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Extensive repainting, replacement storefronts, sign bands, patched masonry, and mixed infill across the row.
photo_observations.notes: This file captures the broader mixed commercial row/block view shown in IMG_20260321_013200818_HDR.jpg.

notes: This file captures the broader mixed commercial row/block view shown in IMG_20260321_013200818_HDR.jpg.

storefront.description: A continuous strip of small retail units runs behind the sidewalk and TTC shelter, with layered illuminated signs, mixed recessed entries, and uneven storefront treatments that read as a heavily altered commercial row.

facade_detail.composition: The street frontage spans roughly 5 metres across 3 storeys beneath a flat roof with a parapet roof, consistent with its commercial typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 5, 8, 8 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 6 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Key storefront and facade features include cornice.

facade_description: 275-289 College St (Jump+ block view) presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 5 metres across 3 storeys beneath a flat roof with a parapet roof, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by mixed, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 5, 8, 8 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 6 principal entrances. Key storefront and facade features include cornice.
